mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
This step installs the Google Cloud SDK, which includes the gcloud command-line tool necessary for managing GKE.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
> Reload the shell to update the environment.
> Initialize the gcloud environment and set up your project.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.31

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations for autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --zone=us-central1-a

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-stackdriver-kubernetes --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Enables cluster autoscaling.
> Enables monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the GKE cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Use --quiet to bypass confirmation prompts.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. The upgrade process will update the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --zone=us-central1-a

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command upgrades the cluster to version 1.31.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --zone=us-central1-a' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user does not have the necessary IAM permissions to perform the action.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your user account has the 'Kubernetes Engine Admin' role assigned.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the nodes are still initializing or if there are issues with the underlying infrastructure.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the nodes with 'kubectl describe nodes' and investigate any reported issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Quota exceeded for project'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the project has hit its resource quota limits.

**Fix:**
Review and adjust your project quotas in the Google Cloud Console.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved workload identity.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy network policies; users should migrate to the new network policy API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that your workloads are compatible with the new PodSecurity standards.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming that your GKE cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured.
